title: Post Fiat Signal Routing Policy Configuration
description: Defines a producer's routing policy: which gates are active, what thresholds apply per symbol, and what weak-symbol interventions are configured. A routing policy is the input to preflight_filter.py alongside candidate signals. Different producers can have different policies while all conforming to the same routing protocol schema.

policy_version.description: Version of this policy configuration. Producers should bump this when thresholds change.

generated_at.description: When this policy was generated or last updated.

evidence_source.description: Where the thresholds came from (e.g. 'proof_surface.json March 26 2026', 'backtest_2492_signals').

gates.description: Gate configuration. Each gate can be enabled/disabled independently. All enabled gates must pass for a signal to be emitted (AND logic). INVERT logic is applied post-gates for configured symbols.

regime_gate.description: Suppresses signals during unfavorable regimes. A regime_id not in the allowed list causes WITHHOLD.

allowed_regimes.description: Regimes in which signals may be emitted. Signals during other regimes are withheld.

duration_gate.description: Suppresses signals when the current regime is too young. Early-regime signals have lower accuracy in backtest evidence (e.g. 38.6% at 10-15d vs 58.9% at 15d+).

default_min_days.description: Default minimum regime duration (days) for all symbols. Per-symbol overrides take precedence.

confidence_gate.description: Suppresses signals with confidence below a minimum. Low-confidence signals have negative expected karma.

default_min_confidence.description: Default minimum confidence for all symbols. Per-symbol overrides take precedence.

voi_gate.description: Suppresses signals with negative expected karma (VOI < threshold). VOI = E[karma|send] - E[karma|withhold]. This is the core decision-theoretic gate: only send if sending adds expected value.

min_voi.description: Minimum VOI threshold. Default 0.0 (only send if positive expected karma). Can be set higher to be more selective.

use_hazard_adjustment.description: Whether to apply Weibull hazard adjustment to VOI (discounts by regime survival probability).

weak_symbol_gate.description: Handles symbols with known weakness. Does NOT withhold by default — instead routes to INVERT when justified, or WITHHOLD when weakness is detected but inversion is not justified.

severity_threshold.description: Minimum weakness severity to trigger intervention. Symbols below this threshold pass through unmodified.

symbols.description: Per-symbol routing configuration. Keys are symbol tickers (uppercase). Symbols not listed use the global defaults from gates.

min_duration_days.description: Override duration gate threshold for this symbol.

min_confidence.description: Override confidence gate threshold for this symbol.

weak_symbol_policy.description: Intervention policy for this symbol. NONE = no intervention. INVERT = flip direction. EXCLUDE = always withhold. REDUCE_WEIGHT = scale confidence by weight_factor.

weight_factor.description: Confidence scaling factor when weak_symbol_policy=REDUCE_WEIGHT. new_confidence = 0.5 + weight_factor * (original - 0.5).

weakness_score.description: Measured weakness score for this symbol (from evaluator).

inversion_justified.description: Whether inversion is statistically justified (CI excludes 50%, n >= 100).

voi_cells.description: Pre-computed VOI by duration bucket for this symbol. Keys are bucket labels (e.g. 'early', 'mid', 'mature', 'late').

description.description: Why this symbol has these overrides.

duration_buckets.description: Duration bucket definitions. Default: early (0-12d), mid (12-15d), mature (15-18d), late (18d+). Used for VOI cell lookup.

weibull_params.description: Weibull parameters per regime for hazard-adjusted VOI. Keys are regime IDs.
